question_title: Will the community prediction be higher than 79.00% on 2026-04-11 for the Metaculus question "Will NASA's Artemis II complete its mission successfully before 2027?"?
summary: This meta-prediction question asks whether the Metaculus CP for Artemis II mission success will exceed 79% on April 11. The dominant factor is that Artemis II is launching TOMORROW (April 1, 2026) — the countdown has begun, weather is 80% favorable, the flight readiness review was unanimously "go", and backup launch windows exist through April 6. The ~10-day mission aligns perfectly with the April 11 check date, meaning if launched and proceeding well, the mission would be nearly complete by then. The decomposition yields: P(launch April 1-6) ≈ 93% × P(mission success) ≈ 89% × P(CP > 79% | success) ≈ 98% = ~81%, plus small contributions from other scenarios. The main NO pathways are mission failure (~10%) and launch window closure (~7%), both of which would push CP well below 79%.

factors.description: Artemis II launch countdown has begun for April 1, with 80% favorable weather, unanimous FRR go-ahead, and backup windows through April 6. A successful launch is the dominant pathway to CP rising above 79%.
If mission launches and goes well for ~10 days, CP would be 90%+ by April 11 (mission nearly complete/completed). The 10-day mission timeline perfectly aligns with the April 11 check date.
CP trend is upward: 75% (Mar 15) → 79% (Mar 29), reflecting growing confidence as launch approaches. Launch imminent should accelerate this trend.
Mission failure risk (~10-12%): First crewed SLS/Orion flight, Manifold 'lethal disaster' at 6.7%, plus non-lethal failure modes. Failure would crash CP well below 79%.
Launch scrub risk (~7%): Persistent technical issue could close entire April 1-6 window. Historical precedent: prior Artemis II scrubs in Feb/March 2026. Delays historically pushed CP to 75% range.
CP is currently EXACTLY at the 79.00% threshold — no buffer. Even small negative developments could keep it at or below threshold.

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.82 (82% probability YES)**

**Key reasoning:**
- Artemis II is launching **tomorrow** (April 1, 2026). The countdown is underway, weather is 80% favorable, and all teams gave unanimous "go" at the flight readiness review.
- The ~10-day mission means it would be nearly complete or completed by the April 11 check date. A successful in-flight or completed mission would push CP well above 79% (likely to 90%+).
- **Main YES pathway** (~81%): Launch proceeds April 1-6, mission goes well → CP surges above 79%.
- **Main NO pathways**: Mission failure/abort after launch (~10%) or persistent technical issue closing entire launch window (~7%), both of which would push CP below 79%.
- Sensitivity analysis across reasonable parameter ranges yields 75-89%, with median near 82%.

resolution_criteria: This question will resolve based on the community prediction of the Metaculus question found [here](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/40864) (i.e. the 'target question'). If the community prediction of the target question on 2026-04-11 03:16:15 is higher than 79.00%, this question will resolve to 'Yes'. If it is lower or equal to 79.00%, this question will resolve to 'No'. If the target question has already resolved before this question opens, then this question will be annulled. If the target question resolves after this question opens, but before 2026-04-11 03:16:15, then this question will resolve to the same value as the target question.
